caveats: a: The BLS occupation mix is much broader than the six-digit industry.
a: Custom industrial producers and commodity mop producers have different task mixes.
rho: Benefits depend on digitized specifications, historical quotes, and material-performance data.
rho: Food, laboratory, medical, and safety applications require accountable validation.
e: The code does not publicly separate professional industrial products from consumer categories.
e: The supplied firm count is an estimate and may include ineligible corporate establishments.
s5: The ABMA membership extends beyond the exact U.S. six-digit lens and includes suppliers.
s5: Broad sale data do not establish qualifying control transfers.
q: Public manufacturer claims do not disclose realized pricing or contracts.
q: Raw-material inflation can obscure productivity retention.
d5: Cleaning-service employment is an indirect product-demand proxy.
d5: Custom industrial brush demand can follow manufacturing cycles rather than cleaning demand.
o: Robotic cleaning still consumes brushes, pads, and replacement tools but may change formats and volumes.
o: Some application support and ordering can become self-service.

lens.customer_and_revenue_model: Revenue is recurring business-to-business supply through standard catalog replenishment, wholesale and private-label programs, specification-driven custom brushes, replacement tools, prototypes, and repeat production runs for industrial, institutional, healthcare, laboratory, and food-service customers.
lens.deviation_from_default: The code combines commodity household cleaning products, artist and cosmetic tools, and highly engineered industrial or medical brushes with materially different pricing and demand. The lens is narrowed to professional B2B industrial and institutional products with repeat supply economics so one operating screen is coherent; the narrowing is based on business-model consistency, not attractiveness.
lens.excluded_activities: Consumer-only household brands, artist and cosmetic brush specialists, direct-to-consumer craft sellers, distributors without manufacturing, captive plants, and platform-scale manufacturers outside the lower middle market.
lens.included_activities: U.S. lower-middle-market manufacturers of industrial, commercial, laboratory, medical, food-service, sanitation, and other professional brushes, brooms, mops, and related custom brushing or cleaning tools supplied repeatedly to external customers.

a.bridge: BLS occupation data measure all miscellaneous manufacturing rather than the narrowed 339994 professional-products lens. I map management, office, sales, business, design, applications, quoting, customer support, planning, procurement, and quality-document work into partial AI exposure while excluding most molding, tufting, winding, trimming, assembly, inspection, packing, and material handling. The broad group includes technical medical manufacturing and may overstate exposure, while custom industrial brush makers may have more applications work than the average.
caveats: The BLS occupation mix is much broader than the six-digit industry.
Custom industrial producers and commodity mop producers have different task mixes.
a.change_evidence: A 339994-specific wage-weighted occupation map and task study across standard and custom professional brush manufacturers would materially narrow the range.
a.rationale: AI can address custom-product configuration, material selection support, quote drafting, order entry, scheduling, purchasing, documentation, and service communications. The core transformation of bristles, wire, fibers, handles, blocks, and mop textiles remains physical and often already machine-assisted.

d5.bridge: BLS projects janitor and building-cleaner employment rather than product shipments, while CDC healthcare-cleaning guidance describes required practices rather than purchases. I translate continuing demand for clean and healthy spaces, modest cleaning-employment growth, healthcare sanitation requirements, and diversified industrial applications into slight five-year real growth. Automation and reusable or high-tech cleaning methods could make the employment proxy overstate product volume.
caveats: Cleaning-service employment is an indirect product-demand proxy.
Custom industrial brush demand can follow manufacturing cycles rather than cleaning demand.
d5.change_evidence: A five-year shipment and consumption forecast for professional brushes, brooms, and mops by janitorial, healthcare, food, laboratory, and industrial end market would materially change the range.
d5.rationale: Brushes, brooms, and mops are consumable or wear items across institutional cleaning and many industrial processes. Healthcare and food sanitation, facility replacement demand, and specialized manufacturing uses are durable, although import competition, product longevity, and robotic cleaning constrain growth.

caveats: The code does not publicly separate professional industrial products from consumer categories.
The supplied firm count is an estimate and may include ineligible corporate establishments.
e.change_evidence: A firm-level map of product category, customer type, repeat-order share, manufacturing status, ownership, and normalized EBITDA would materially change the estimate.
e.rationale: Professional catalog replenishment, private-label production, and custom industrial programs fit the recurring external-customer lens. The code also contains consumer brands, art and cosmetic specialists, distributors, corporate plants, and firms outside the LMM band, reducing the eligible share under the narrowed lens.

l.detail: QCEW 2024 private total annual wages at 339994 ($500,375,930) x benefits multiplier 1.4294 [ECEC private-industry total comp $46.60/hr / wages $32.60/hr = 1.4294 (March 2026 reference period)] / SUSB 2022 receipts at 339994 ($3,001,506,000). Vintage mismatch: 2024 wages over 2022 receipts, stated. Level: 6-digit. Harmonized to IPS basis ÷1.3407 (median of 65 dual-method codes).

n.detail: margin 0.1962 (margins.json[33]: Damodaran Jan 2026: Machinery EBITDA/Sales 19.62%); revenue/employee $334,095 (SUSB 2022 receipts/employment at 339994); class <5: 31 firms, avg revenue/firm $447,387 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $87,777 -> out of $1-10M band; class 5-9: 25 firms, avg revenue/firm $1,576,520 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $309,313 -> out of $1-10M band; class 10-19: 25 firms, avg revenue/firm $2,978,400 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $584,362 -> out of $1-10M band; class 20-99: 31 firms, avg revenue/firm $10,196,935 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $2,000,639 -> IN $1-10M band; class 100-499: 17 firms, avg revenue/firm $58,011,412 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $11,381,839 -> out of $1-10M band; class 500+: 9 firms, avg revenue/firm $174,607,222 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $34,257,937 -> out of $1-10M band; n_band = 31

caveats: Robotic cleaning still consumes brushes, pads, and replacement tools but may change formats and volumes.
Some application support and ordering can become self-service.
o.change_evidence: Evidence on robotic cleaning adoption, customers' in-house brush fabrication, self-service configuration, and process redesign that eliminates brushing or mopping would materially change the estimate.
o.rationale: Customers still require physical bristles, fibers, mop heads, handles, blocks, and custom brushing tools. AI can improve self-service design, ordering, and troubleshooting but cannot deliver the cleaning, surface-treatment, or material-handling function as software.

q.bridge: Manufacturer pages document quoted custom designs, prototyping, thousands of catalog SKUs, rapid turnaround, exact specifications, repair, and private labeling, but do not measure retained AI benefit. I infer greater retention for custom, application-critical, low-volume, or fast-turn work and less for standard janitorial products subject to wholesale bidding and imports. The proxy may overstate retention if a target is weighted toward commodity mops or brooms.
caveats: Public manufacturer claims do not disclose realized pricing or contracts.
Raw-material inflation can obscure productivity retention.
q.change_evidence: Gross-margin bridges following measured workflow savings, segmented by custom industrial, standard catalog, private label, healthcare, and janitorial products, would materially change the estimate.
q.rationale: Application expertise, specification fit, speed, domestic production, and custom tooling support differentiation. Standard cleaning tools are readily comparable, and institutional procurement or private-label buyers can force savings through at rebid.

caveats: Benefits depend on digitized specifications, historical quotes, and material-performance data.
Food, laboratory, medical, and safety applications require accountable validation.
rho.change_evidence: Measured pilots at representative firms covering custom-quote time, product selection, customer-service resolution, scheduling, and document preparation would materially change the estimate.
rho.rationale: Within five years, LMM operators can implement AI for quote intake, specification matching, product and material lookup, prototype documentation, order entry, customer support, forecasting, purchasing, and production planning. Legacy product masters, fragmented drawings, physical prototyping, sanitation rules, and quality review limit realized adoption.

s5.bridge: BizBuySell covers broad manufacturing sales and Census owner-characteristics data cover the economy, not qualifying 339994 transfers. I use those sources, together with ABMA's evidence of a long-standing small and family-run manufacturer base, to infer moderate five-year succession and sale probability. Broad transaction data may overstate transfers for specialized producers with customer-specific know-how, while family ownership may raise succession supply.
caveats: The ABMA membership extends beyond the exact U.S. six-digit lens and includes suppliers.
Broad sale data do not establish qualifying control transfers.
s5.change_evidence: A member-level ownership map, owner-age survey, and database of closed brush, broom, and mop manufacturing control transactions would materially change the range.
s5.rationale: The trade association describes a mature base of small, closely held companies, which supports succession potential, but control-transfer evidence for this specific industry is not published.

principal_driver: Recurring replenishment and repeat custom designs create reusable product, quote, and customer-history data across a long tail of professional applications.
principal_weakness: Commodity products and a low labor-cost share limit retained AI economics unless the target has defensible custom or specification-driven revenue.

sources.supports: Defines the industry as establishments primarily manufacturing brooms, mops, and brushes.
sources.title: 2022 NAICS definition: 339994 Broom, Brush, and Mop Manufacturing

sources.supports: Provides the broad occupation mix used to proxy wage-weighted AI task exposure.
sources.title: May 2023 Occupational Employment and Wage Estimates: NAICS 339000 Miscellaneous Manufacturing

sources.supports: Documents engineered and repaired brushes, exact specifications, diverse industrial applications, and customer productivity use cases.
sources.title: Cocker-Weber Brush Company: Custom Industrial Brushes

sources.supports: Documents more than 17,000 specialty and industrial brushes, custom design, private labeling, rapid turnaround, and industry-specific technical resources.
sources.title: Gordon Brush: U.S. Wholesale Industrial Brush Manufacturer

sources.supports: Shows more than 2,500 standard and specialty brushes, custom design, U.S. production, and laboratory, medical, scientific, industrial, and food-service markets.
sources.title: Justman Brush Company

sources.supports: Documents a long-standing North American broom, brush, and mop manufacturing community, industry data programs, innovation, global competition, and a base of small and family-run companies.
sources.title: American Brush Manufacturers Association

sources.supports: Provides broad manufacturing transaction benchmarks for 2021-2025, used only as a control-transfer proxy.
sources.title: BizBuySell Manufacturing Business Valuation Benchmarks

sources.supports: Provides economy-wide owner-age and ownership-characteristics tables used only as a succession proxy.
sources.title: 2022 Annual Business Survey: Characteristics of Business Owners

sources.supports: Projects 2% employment growth from 2024 to 2034, 351,300 annual openings, and continuing need for clean and healthy spaces while noting high-tech methods may limit growth.
sources.title: BLS Occupational Outlook Handbook: Janitors and Building Cleaners

sources.supports: Documents recurring environmental cleaning and disinfection practices, including explicit procedures for mops, cloths, surfaces, and medical equipment.
sources.title: CDC Recommendations for Environmental Infection Control in Health-Care Facilities

title: Broom, Brush, and Mop Manufacturing
